item.title: Classification of Soccer Player Position by Numeric Metrics
item.description: Introduction As both of us are have played soccer for most of our lives, we thought it would be interesting to look at characteristics of professional soccer players around the world. We looked at 3 datasets from Kaggle. The first, called Soccer_Stats looks at numeric metrics in the 2015-2016 Premier League season for each FIFA player, such as overall rating, preferred foot, crossing, finishing, standing tackle, etc. We selected the data to only look at 12 key metrics, namely crossing’, ‘finishing’, ‘dribbling’, ‘sprint_speed’, ‘stamina’, ‘strength’,‘aggression’, ‘vision’, ‘marking’, ‘standing_tackle’, ‘player_name’, ‘Position’, ‘Appearances’ The second dataset called “player_pos” looks at variables such as team, position, appearances, goals, etc.

item.title: Projects
item.description: Check out some of my coding projects below: Wrangling & Exploratory Data Analysis Modeling, Inference, Prediction --

item.title: The Correlation Between Weather and Crime
item.description: Introduction I have used the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Association(NOAA) weather dataset and Chicago Crime Dataset from the City of Chicago. I chose these because they both have daily entries, and I was able to merge the datasets by day. These will allow to me find a day by day relationship between weather and crime, as I felt that monthly was too far apart to find meaningful relationships. I also liked the crime dataset because it labels the type of each crime.
